description.content: Top-level Manager object methods.
Reloads all unit files from disk without restarting services. Mirrors `Reload()`.
Re-executes the systemd binary in-place, preserving state. Mirrors `Reexecute()`.
Creates the symlinks that make units start at boot. Mirrors `EnableUnitFiles(files, runtime, force)`.
Removes the symlinks that make units start at boot. Mirrors `DisableUnitFiles(files, runtime)`.
Symlinks units to /dev/null so they cannot be started. Mirrors `MaskUnitFiles(files, runtime, force)`.
Reverses MaskUnitFiles. Mirrors `UnmaskUnitFiles(files, runtime)`.
Mirrors `SetDefaultTarget(target, force)`.
Mirrors `GetDefaultTarget()`.
Returns the full property map of the Manager (Version, Features, Virtualization, Architecture, Tainted, FirmwareTimestamp, etc.).
Unit lifecycle and enumeration.
Returns an array of all currently loaded units. Mirrors `ListUnits()` on the Manager.

response.name: Array of units (name, description, load state, active state, sub state, follower, object path, job id, job type, job object path).

item.name: Get A Loaded Unit By Name

request.name: Get A Loaded Unit By Name

description.content: Returns the D-Bus object path of an already-loaded unit. Mirrors `GetUnit(name)`.
Forces the Manager to load the named unit and returns its object path. Mirrors `LoadUnit(name)`.
Enqueues a start job for the unit. Mirrors `StartUnit(name, mode)`. Mode is one of `replace`, `fail`, `isolate`, `ignore-dependencies`, `ignore-requirements`.

response.name: Job enqueued; returns job object path.

description.content: Enqueues a stop job. Mirrors `StopUnit(name, mode)`.
Stops then starts. Mirrors `RestartUnit(name, mode)`.
Asks a unit to reload its configuration (e.g. SIGHUP for services). Mirrors `ReloadUnit(name, mode)`.
Reloads if supported, otherwise restarts. Mirrors `ReloadOrRestartUnit(name, mode)`.

item.name: Send A Signal To A Unit

request.name: Send A Signal To A Unit

description.content: Sends a UNIX signal to the unit's main, control, or all processes. Mirrors `KillUnit(name, who, signal)`.
Suspends all processes in the unit's cgroup using the cgroup v2 freezer. Mirrors `FreezeUnit(name)`.
Resumes a frozen cgroup. Mirrors `ThawUnit(name)`.

item.name: Get All Properties Of A Unit

request.name: Get All Properties Of A Unit

description.content: Returns the full property bag for a unit. Equivalent to `org.freedesktop.DBus.Properties.GetAll`.

item.name: Set Runtime Properties On A Unit

request.name: Set Runtime Properties On A Unit

description.content: Sets one or more properties at runtime. Mirrors `SetUnitProperties(name, runtime, properties)`. Used for live cgroup resource changes (CPUWeight, MemoryMax, etc.).
Creates and starts a new transient unit (typically a scope or service) with the supplied properties. Mirrors `StartTransientUnit(name, mode, properties, aux)`.
Pending and active jobs scheduled by the Manager.
Mirrors `ListJobs()`.
Mirrors `CancelJob(id)`.
Loading, reloading, and reexecuting the manager.
Reloads all unit files from disk without restarting services. Mirrors `Reload()`.
Re-executes the systemd binary in-place, preserving state. Mirrors `Reexecute()`.
Creates the symlinks that make units start at boot. Mirrors `EnableUnitFiles(files, runtime, force)`.
Removes the symlinks that make units start at boot. Mirrors `DisableUnitFiles(files, runtime)`.
Symlinks units to /dev/null so they cannot be started. Mirrors `MaskUnitFiles(files, runtime, force)`.
Reverses MaskUnitFiles. Mirrors `UnmaskUnitFiles(files, runtime)`.
Mirrors `SetDefaultTarget(target, force)`.
Mirrors `GetDefaultTarget()`.
Transient unit creation and cgroup-backed resource control.
Creates and starts a new transient unit (typically a scope or service) with the supplied properties. Mirrors `StartTransientUnit(name, mode, properties, aux)`.
System state introspection.
Returns the full property map of the Manager (Version, Features, Virtualization, Architecture, Tainted, FirmwareTimestamp, etc.).

This OpenAPI document models the well-known systemd D-Bus interface set at bus name
`org.freedesktop.systemd1` (system bus). It is a documentation/contract artifact: callers do not actually
issue HTTPS requests against a server, they invoke D-Bus methods against PID 1. Paths here mirror the
canonical object path layout (`/org/freedesktop/systemd1/...`) and the operationId names match the
documented D-Bus method names.

Bus name: `org.freedesktop.systemd1`. Root object path: `/org/freedesktop/systemd1`. The Manager
interface enumerates units, manages jobs, loads/reloads configuration, and emits lifecycle signals.
Per-unit objects implement the `Unit` interface plus a type-specific interface (`Service`, `Socket`,
`Mount`, `Slice`, `Scope`, `Path`, `Swap`, `Timer`, `Target`).
